summary: The most likely diagnosis is dysgerminoma because it is the most common ovarian malignancy in young females under age 25 and commonly associates with moderately elevated LDH levels due to its high proliferative nature. Among other choices, endodermal sinus tumors also may present with raised LDH but are rareer in this demographic.
